sections.overview: The Mahila Bakri Palan scheme was introduced by the Department of Animal Husbandry, Government of Uttarakhand to support women through goat rearing for livelihood generation and improved household income.
sections.objective: To empower women, especially abandoned, widowed, destitute, single, and disaster-affected women, by providing financial support for goat rearing.
eligibility: Applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand
Applicant must belong to one of the following categories: abandoned, widow, destitute, single, or disaster-affected
benefits: One unit of goats consisting of 3 females and 1 male aged 12 to 18 months is provided
Total cost of ₹35,000 per unit is fully borne by the State Government
Funds are transferred to the beneficiary’s bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT)
application_process: Beneficiaries are selected on a first come, first served basis
Applicants must submit their proposal in an open meeting of the Gram Sabha
Proposal should be submitted to the Veterinary Officer, Livestock Extension Officer, or Gram Panchayat Development Officer
Applicants must submit Aadhaar card, bank details, and relevant supporting documents along with the proposal

faq.question: Who are eligible for this scheme?
faq.answer: Abandoned, widowed, destitute, single, and disaster-affected women are eligible for this scheme.

faq.question: What support is provided under the scheme?
faq.answer: One unit of goats consisting of 3 females and 1 male aged 12 to 18 months is provided.

faq.question: Who bears the cost of the scheme?
faq.answer: The entire cost of ₹35,000 per unit is borne by the State Government of Uttarakhand.

faq.question: How can women apply for this scheme?
faq.answer: Applicants must submit their proposal in an open meeting of the Gram Sabha.

faq.question: To whom should the proposal be submitted?
faq.answer: The proposal should be submitted to the Veterinary Officer, Livestock Extension Officer, or Gram Panchayat Development Officer of the area.

faq.question: How are funds provided to beneficiaries?
faq.answer: Funds are transferred to the beneficiary’s bank account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) within one to two months after selection.

faq.question: On what basis are beneficiaries selected?
faq.answer: Beneficiaries are selected on a first come, first served basis.
